plan.reasoning: This ticket requests a new marketing website for the SprintFoundry product. The requirements are clear and detailed with specific sections defined, so no product agent is needed. The work is purely frontend/UI with a new folder structure, requiring UI/UX design followed by developer implementation and QA validation.

steps.task: Design the SprintFoundry marketing website for the website-v1 folder. Create component specs and wireframes for: (1) Hero section with animated agent workflow visualization showing agents collaborating, (2) Features section highlighting 6 agent types (Product, Architecture, Developer, QA, Security, DevOps) each with a distinct accent color, (3) How It Works section illustrating the ticket-to-PR pipeline as a step-by-step flow, (4) Pricing section with Free/Pro/Enterprise tiers and feature comparison, (5) Modern dark theme design system with agent-specific accent colors. Define the color palette, typography, component hierarchy, and animation specifications.

Build the SprintFoundry marketing website in a new website-v1 folder. Use Next.js with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS. Implement: (1) Hero section with CSS/Framer Motion animated visualization of agents receiving a ticket and producing a PR, (2) Features section with 6 agent cards (Product, Architecture, Developer, QA, Security, DevOps) each with distinct accent colors — use purple for product, blue for architect, green for developer, yellow for QA, red for security, orange for DevOps, (3) How It Works section showing the 5-step ticket-to-PR pipeline with connecting arrows/flow, (4) Pricing section with three tiers: Free (solo devs, 10 tickets/mo), Pro ($49/mo, 200 tickets, all agents), Enterprise (custom, unlimited, SSO), (5) Dark theme (#0a0a0f background, agent accent colors for highlights), responsive layout, smooth scroll navigation header. Initialize a standalone Next.js project with its own package.json inside website-v1/.

Validate the SprintFoundry marketing website in website-v1. Check: (1) All required sections are present (hero, features, how-it-works, pricing), (2) All 6 agent types are represented in the features section with distinct styling, (3) Pricing section has all three tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise), (4) The site builds without errors (run npm run build), (5) Responsive layout renders correctly at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1440px) breakpoints, (6) No console errors or missing imports, (7) Dark theme is consistently applied, (8) Navigation links are functional. Report any bugs found.

data.reason: Pricing spec conflict needs product decision: original task spec ($49/mo, 10 tickets) vs UI spec ($79/mo, unlimited tickets). Additionally, missing focus trap is an accessibility regression vs the stated requirements in dev-to-qa.md.

data.task: Resolve the pricing spec conflict: the original task spec states $49/mo with 10 tickets for the Pro tier, but the UI spec artifact used by the developer specifies $79/mo with unlimited tickets. Review both artifacts (artifacts/product-spec.md and the UI/UX component specifications) and issue a definitive pricing decision document that establishes the canonical pricing for all three tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise) including price points, ticket limits, and feature inclusions. This will be the authoritative source for the developer to update the implementation.

Fix three issues in the SprintFoundry marketing website (website-v1): (1) Update Pricing section to match the canonical pricing confirmed by the product agent in step 901 — update price points, ticket limits, and feature copy for all three tiers; (2) Add an onClick handler or href to the 'Watch Demo' button in HeroSection so clicking it performs a meaningful action (e.g., scrolls to the how-it-works section or opens a modal placeholder); (3) Implement a focus trap for the mobile navigation drawer so that Tab/Shift+Tab key navigation is constrained within the open drawer, satisfying the accessibility spec in dev-to-qa.md. Verify the build still passes after changes.

Validate the SprintFoundry marketing website in website-v1. Check: (1) All required sections are present (hero, features, how-it-works, pricing), (2) All 6 agent types are represented in the features section with distinct styling, (3) Pricing section has all three tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise), (4) The site builds without errors (run npm run build), (5) Responsive layout renders correctly at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1440px) breakpoints, (6) No console errors or missing imports, (7) Dark theme is consistently applied, (8) Navigation links are functional. Report any bugs found.

data.reason: MAJOR bug found: All three pricing CTA buttons (Start Building Free, Start Free Trial, Contact Sales) navigate to '#' instead of the correct destinations specified in product spec US-3. This means clicking any pricing CTA causes the page to scroll to the top rather than routing users to signup or contact flows.

data.task: Fix pricing CTA button hrefs in PricingSection.tsx: change the Free tier button href from '#' to '/signup', the Pro tier button href from '#' to '/signup?plan=pro', and the Enterprise tier button href from '#' to '/contact'. These are the spec-required destinations per product spec US-3 (see PricingSection.tsx lines 27, 45, and 65). Verify the build still passes after the change.

Re-validate the SprintFoundry marketing website in website-v1 after the pricing CTA href fix. Specifically verify: (1) Free tier CTA navigates to /signup, (2) Pro tier CTA navigates to /signup?plan=pro, (3) Enterprise tier CTA navigates to /contact, (4) all previously passing tests still pass, (5) build is clean. Report any remaining issues.

Validate the SprintFoundry marketing website in website-v1. Check: (1) All required sections are present (hero, features, how-it-works, pricing), (2) All 6 agent types are represented in the features section with distinct styling, (3) Pricing section has all three tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise), (4) The site builds without errors (run npm run build), (5) Responsive layout renders correctly at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1440px) breakpoints, (6) No console errors or missing imports, (7) Dark theme is consistently applied, (8) Navigation links are functional. Report any bugs found.
